text: The Migdal stone—a temple artifact likely associated with Jesus's era—contains an encoded model of the astrological universe with precise planetary positions from April 17, 6 BCE, demonstrating that Greek astronomical knowledge was embedded in Jewish cultic practice.
quote: the migdal stone models a universe that represents exactly the same planetary positions and sky that happened on April 17th 6 BCE...the menorah literally represents all the Heavenly lights occupying only the constellations Taurus Aries and Pisces

text: The speaker identifies Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Susannah as financial supporters of Jesus's movement who had direct connections to the Herodian family—specifically, one was the wife of Herod Antipas's chief of staff. This demonstrates Mary Magdalene's integration into Hellenistic court structures, strengthening the book's framework for understanding early Christian figures as embedded within Greco-institutional contexts.
quote: three of his main followers who actually supported his movement financially were mary magdalene joanna and susannah and they were connected to the herodian family and one of them was the wife of herod's chief of staff this is herod antipas
